← Back to Blog Safety

How to Avoid the 7 Most Common Alibaba Scams

September 20, 2024 ·7 min read ·CrossPals Team

Alibaba is used by millions of legitimate businesses worldwide. But it also hosts middlemen, fraudsters, and low-quality operators.

1. The Too-Good-to-Be-True Price

If a supplier's price is 30–50% below every other listing, something is wrong. Use it as reference only.

2. Moving Off Platform

A supplier who asks to negotiate on WhatsApp bypasses Trade Assurance. Never pay outside the platform on a first order.

3. Sample Bait-and-Switch

Excellent sample, terrible bulk order. Always specify bulk goods must match the approved sample — and inspect before releasing payment.

4. Fake Gold Supplier Badges

Gold Supplier status just means they paid for a subscription. Look at transaction history and reviews instead.

5. Inflated Shipping Quotes

Some suppliers make hidden margin on shipping. Always get an independent freight quote to compare.

6. The Deposit Disappearance

Never pay 100% upfront to a new supplier. Standard terms: 30% deposit, 70% after inspection.

7. Counterfeit Certificates

CE, ISO certificates are easy to fake. Ask for the certificate number and verify — or let CrossPals verify for you.
